direction.current_focus: Keep operator continuity exact and clean while rebuilding the world system around canon, simulation, and embodiment.
direction.why_now: The old stack became bloated because memory, planning, and world state were all mixed together.

delivery.rule: A PR should close one todo item id or one clearly coupled pair. If work spills into a different goal, split it. Once the item is clean and green, merge it to main immediately and delete the work branch.

items.title: Finish exact Hermes operator memory adoption and remove any remaining fake memory sources.
items.why: Operator continuity has to be exact and singular before anything else can be trusted.
items.done_when: The vendored Hermes memory module is the only operator memory implementation and the tests stay clean.

items.title: Keep one simple todo board that can survive direction changes without getting garbled.
items.why: Planning drift is how the repo becomes sloppy again.
items.done_when: There is one validated board with clear now, next, and later work and a second-agent review can judge it cleanly.

items.title: Design the canon engine so world truth stays separate from operator memory and runtime beliefs.
items.why: World continuity is still unsolved until canon has its own home.
items.done_when: Canon facts, approvals, and runtime beliefs have separate boundaries.

items.title: Build the simulation runtime that reads canon without overwriting it.
items.why: The living world should emerge from canon instead of replacing it.
items.done_when: Runtime state can evolve scene by scene while canon stays authoritative.

items.title: Build the player-facing layer for entering the living world.
items.why: The user insertion layer only makes sense after canon and simulation are stable.
items.done_when: The system can place the user into a persistent scene without confusing builder state with world state.
